storyInfo: Lena has returned to Athenius and is seeking help to establish a system for Oripathy treatment in her homeland, but an old face stands in her way.

attributes.content: You got the workers and farmers to calm down by having them arm-wrestle as Heraces did? You are a clever one, Titi.
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: But you already found the solution last night. You just fell asleep in a pile of apples due to exhaustion.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: Shame about the apple cider... you were planning to treat the workers and farmers, and get them to work together after they got a little tipsy, weren't you?
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: Correct. But it's being put to good use to treat an honored guest.
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: No better medicine for boat sickness, if you're still feeling it! Yia mas!
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: Yia mas!
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: Oh, so sweet—this is good!
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: Please, tell me honestly... even though we had the same idea, was it perhaps inappropriate for me to interfere with the rebuilding of the First Temple?
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: Because you're the Sargonian ambassador?
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: Because Sargon was—
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: Just answer this question, Titi. Do you think the Whitemane King's conquest of Minos was necessary?
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: No.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: Even though it distracted Sargonians from internal strife?
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: The empire needs to solve its own issues! Blaming it on innocent neighbors is a sign of incompetence.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: Although I probably wouldn't be so frank, back in Sargon...
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: Fine words, fine words! Have another drink! Yamas!
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: I didn't think a woman so carefree and whimsical would be so pointed when discussing history.
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: As long as your answer comes from the heart and not concern for your own safety, I think we'll become very good friends.
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: Priestess Pallas—
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: Is this how you address a friend?
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: Then... Just Pallas. I believe one must not forget oneself, no matter where one goes.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: Pepe—a friend of mine back in Sargon—says my train of thought is all over the place and I have no common sense, but that's another matter entirely!
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: Looks like the Sargon padishahs made a wise choice in picking you to lead the mission...
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: Don't worry, the workers and farmers are very fond of you. We know a bad egg when we see one, and we don't hate all Sargonians.
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: Besides, at a time like this, simply participating in the rebuilding of the First Temple is hardly crossing any lines, as long as it helps solve problems.
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: What do you mean?
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: Look at the pillars, reliefs, and decorations inside the temple. Most of the details are unfinished. The amphitheater outside? There's no slope with the audience seats, so anyone behind the third row can't see what's going on in the center.
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: And the Kereseira statue that's supposed to be in the middle of the theater? It's still being polished by its perfectionist sculptor.
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: That's...
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: A huge-ass problem. No need to weigh your words—it's not like we're writing poetry. Everyone knows that our progress has been way behind schedule.
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: Have another drink. Opa!
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: Now, there's no hard-and-fast deadline. Things will get done eventually, even if the pursuit of perfection keeps delaying things. But a lot of people will be affected, if the delays continue.
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: The orchard harvests...
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: Yes. Arm-wrestling and cider might not solve things next time... speaking of cider, you're going through it quickly. Want another cup?
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: Please! I do have a question, though.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: What is it?
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: You help the craftsmen solve their problems, and they rely on you. But you don't look concerned at all, even though you're supposed to be the project's supervisor.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: Hm? No, I wasn't ever the supervisor.
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: Say what now?
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: The old tradition only calls for a priest, in this case Kassandra, to give the go-ahead. There is no supervisor for the project itself.
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: It's not a job for an idle drunk like me. However...
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: You look like a reasonable gal, and you catch on quick.
attributes.name: Pallas
Titi
attributes.content: (Takes another swill) Mm...

attributes.name: Titi
attributes.content: What did you say? I was distracted...
No, nothing.
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: You went through a whole bottle already? You sure can hold your drink. My brew's pretty strong, you know.
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: Something's not right... didn't you say this cider would help with my boat sickness? I only seem to get dizzier and dizzier...
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: Hey, booze dizziness beats water dizziness, right? Drink until you pass out, and everything will be fine when you wake up the next morning. Including a bit of boat sickness.
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: Whuh?
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: I'm sorry, Father, Mother. I've been infected with Oripathy. Your line of work would never let an Infected take over, so now, I'm free to do what I want, right?
attributes.name: Lena

attributes.content: I know I can't stay at home any longer. I'll move out as soon as I can. Don't worry about me, I'm able to care for myself now, and I don't need to trouble my family for anything.
attributes.name: Lena

attributes.content: But if it's alright, could you leave my flower garden where it is? They're just plants. Oripathy won't taint them.
attributes.name: Lena

attributes.content: Please, let me say goodbye.
attributes.name: Lena

attributes.content: It's been a long time since I last came back to Athenius. Did I forget the way?
attributes.name: Lena

attributes.content: The public garden west of the agora... I should be in the right place. Where's Kassandra?
attributes.name: Lena

attributes.content: Did you think a member of the Athenian Council would meet privately with a Rhodes Islander in a public place?
Looks like you've been away for so long that you've forgotten how things work here.
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: Lydia? It was you who wanted to see me?
attributes.name: Lena

attributes.content: I heard you're the Astynomos of Athenius now? I had wanted to catch up with you sometime, but setting up the Rhodes Island branch office took so much time—
attributes.name: Lena

attributes.content: I know. You've done a lot of things for the Infected in the two months that you've been back in Athenius. You could hardly have found the time to come home.
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: I don't plan on going home, Lydia. They...
attributes.name: Lena

attributes.content: Don't worry, you wouldn't run into those vain relatives, even if you went back to the estate.
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: What is it?
attributes.name: Lena

attributes.content: Two years ago, the Athopoulou family declared bankruptcy and quit the herb business. The remaining family members have since moved out of Athenius.
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: I see...
attributes.name: Lena

attributes.content: Looks like you really don't care anymore.
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: There isn't much worth remembering about the place, other than my garden.
attributes.name: Lena

attributes.content: Your mother used to bring you to play with me in the garden. You came to our house after she passed away.
attributes.name: Lena

attributes.content: I taught you about plants and their fragrances. You showed little interest, but you planted the blue boronias with me together, just like the ones here...
attributes.name: Lena

attributes.content: You've really forgotten.
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: Wait... don't tell me, this public garden...
attributes.name: Lena

attributes.content: This is the flowerbed where you studied and planted medicinal herbs.
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: Your parents sold the land to the Athenian government to pay off their debts, soon after I became Astynomos.
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: Did you think they would protect it? Honor the request of the daughter they exiled?
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: You've changed, Lydia.
attributes.name: Lena

attributes.content: So have you.
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: Visiting the Infected, contacting the healing priests, and even reaching out to Kassandra to make sure that veneration of traditional heroism by the Infected would not get in the way of their treatment.
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: Handling procedure used to be your great weakness. Now, you've learned to circumvent the parts of city administration where the power structure is yet unclear, and go over the head of the new Astynomos.
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: I see. You didn't come to reminisce about old times. You want to prevent me from talking to the priests—
attributes.name: Lena

attributes.content: To take the authority to establish and maintain the Infected medical system from the Athenian Council and give it to the Astynomos.
attributes.name: Lena

attributes.content: You picked up on that fast.
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: It wouldn't be the first time Rhodes Island has dealt with a power-hungry official.
attributes.name: Lena

attributes.content: What Rhodes Island does in an unofficial capacity is none of my business, be it making visits or distributing medicine. You're already doing it, without communication with the Astynomos.
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: But you know that changes are coming to Minos, be it the rebuilding of the First Temple, the visit of the Sargonian mission, or the establishment of an Oripathy treatment system.
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: The legends of the Minoan heroes are in our blood, but the same traditions have also brought about convoluted governance, inefficient systems, and backward thinking.
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: You know that better than anyone else.
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: I want to say you can do whatever you want, as long as an Oripathy treatment system is established, and my patients get what they need.
attributes.name: Lena

attributes.content: But you're the only one in the family I still care about. You're my sister, and I don't want you to put yourself in danger.
attributes.name: Lena

attributes.content: Looks like our dear 'ambassador' actually did go strolling off to visit the temple construction site.
attributes.name: Behnui

attributes.content: All's well, I suppose. She'll be a fine distraction while I dig into this Astynomos' background. Is the ceremony really delayed, or is she intentionally stalling? If she shows us any disrespect...
attributes.name: Behnui

attributes.content: Make the preparations. We're going to the Astynomos' Office.
attributes.name: Behnui

attributes.content: What? Who's this girl? Guards!
attributes.name: Behnui

attributes.content: Beg your pardon, Your Excellency. This is my traveling companion. We hail from Corinia and Lachedamon, invited to attend the ceremony as guests.
attributes.name: Friendly Tourist

attributes.content: She's passionate about Sargonian culture and insisted on catching a glimpse of the ambassador upon hearing that the mission is lodging here. I couldn't stop her.
attributes.name: Friendly Tourist

attributes.content: The ambassador... ahem. You have sharp eyes.
attributes.name: Behnui

attributes.content: You were invited to attend, though? I haven't heard anything from the Astynomos. I understand that the venue of the exchange ceremony has not been confirmed.
attributes.name: Behnui

attributes.content: Ah, I shouldn't have babbled, then. Perhaps Lydia intended it to be a surprise.
attributes.name: Friendly Tourist

attributes.content: I am Períandros, priest of Corinia. We learned about the diplomatic mission to Athenius six months ago.
attributes.name: Períandros

attributes.content: Six months? That long ago?
attributes.name: Behnui

attributes.content: Yes. The climate of Sargon is very different from that of Minos. Lydia asked me to procure Sargonian-made goods from Corinia in order to make you and the rest of the delegation feel at home.
attributes.name: Períandros

attributes.content: I hope you are enjoying your accommodations.
attributes.name: Períandros

attributes.content: Ah, so this was your handiwork.
attributes.name: Behnui

attributes.content: Lydia has devoted tremendous time and effort to ensuring that the ceremony is a success.
attributes.name: Períandros

attributes.content: Unfortunately, some of the fabric meant for the costumes for the play have yet to make their way to Hiéreia Kassandra, resulting in a significant delay. Did she mention anything?
attributes.name: Períandros

attributes.content: A play... the Astynomos did mention that plays are a part of every important occasion.
attributes.name: Behnui

attributes.content: But who is this Hiéreia Kassandra? I thought this matter was entirely managed by the Astynomos' Office.
attributes.name: Behnui

attributes.content: Ah, it all makes sense now! I almost misunderstood what she meant. No wonder she left specific instructions not to use the traditional fabric for classical plays. I'm so grateful for the heads-up.
attributes.name: Períandros

attributes.content: The cotton used for this event is a hybrid of Sargonian and Minoan species. They make a smooth and strong cloth that signifies the lasting peace between our two nations. That's why Lydia insisted on waiting.
attributes.name: Períandros

attributes.content: I see. So this is the reason she delayed the ceremony...
attributes.name: Behnui

attributes.content: Delayed? I'm so sorry about that. I really apologize for the trouble. I hope you will not take Lydia's best intentions the wrong way.
attributes.name: Períandros

attributes.content: Very well. It would be impolite to rush her, if she is taking this ceremony so seriously. Thank you for bringing this to my attention.
attributes.name: Behnui

attributes.content: You're too kind. Please, make yourselves at home for a few days while preparations for the ceremony continue.
attributes.name: Períandros

attributes.content: Don't look at me like that. I came with you when you made three rounds of the food stalls in the morning. Only fair that you should come with me here.
attributes.name: Períandros

attributes.content: How much of what you said was actually true?
attributes.name: Stray Tourist

attributes.content: The Sargonian delegation has entered the city, and the Astynomos invited us to attend the ceremony, but she didn't tell us anything about her arrangements. What choice do I have but to figure this out on my own?
attributes.name: Períandros

attributes.content: I assuaged his concerns for her, didn't I?
attributes.name: Períandros

attributes.content: The merchants of Corinia would not be famous throughout Terra, if profit were the only thing on their minds.
attributes.name: Stray Tourist

attributes.content: Now, now, it's not polite to say that out loud.
attributes.name: Períandros
